content:
4 min read
3 hours ago

--

Six months after my first post, ArachnoScript is leveling up dramatically. Here’s everything new in the v0.2 series and why v0.2.5 is a game-changer.

Six months ago, I shared my journey building **ArachnoScript** — a JavaScript-inspired language with deliberate differences designed for safety, clarity, and real-world usability. What started as an experiment has grown into a steadily maturing project with a dedicated runtime (ARE) written in Go.
Today, I’m excited to give you a full update on the v0.2 series and preview **v0.2.5** — one of the biggest leaps yet. Many features originally planned for v0.3 are landing early. Let’s dive in.
### Why ArachnoScript Exists
ArachnoScript isn’t trying to replace JavaScript. It’s built to **complement** it.
It keeps a familiar, JS-like syntax while introducing intentional differences:
- Function parameters are **deep-copied by default** (no more accidental mutations from the caller).
- Arrays are lightweight, non-object types (closer to C/Go behavior), but the ArachnoScript standard library still provides the language with a similar ‘Array’ constructor.
- Explicit runtime types, strong immutability options, and no prototype pollution.
- Support for classes with inheritance/private/public fields/getters/setters, async/await, match expressions, and more.
The result? A language that feels familiar but behaves more predictably for backend, scripting, and full-stack work.
### The v0.2 Journey So Far
**v0.2.0 (April 2026)** kicked things off with a major foundational overhaul:
- Complete lexer/parser rewrite (moved away from heavy regex to a cleaner precedence-based system).
- Improved interpreter, error messages, destructuring, imports, classes, and the event loop.
- Stronger standard library foundations.
**v0.2.1 (May 2026)** focused on syntax polish and developer experience:
- Updated `for` loop syntax for clarity (now requires explicit `spawn`/`let` in the initializer).
- Better CLI (`are run`, `are -e “code”` for quick evaluation).
- New macros and modules (HTTP, regexp, paths, etc.).
Subsequent patches (up to **v0.2.12-alpha**) delivered steady refinements: parser tweaks, array/I/O fixes, new macros like `#_sleep` and date helpers, expanded stdlib (encoding, date modules), and numerous stability improvements.
### 🚀 v0.2.5: The Big Leap (Coming Soon)
v0.2.5 pulls forward major advancements and represents one of the most significant updates yet. Here’s what’s new:
- **New Parser (0.2.5-genesis-1)**: A complete overhaul producing a much lighter and more efficient AST. Expect faster interpretation, fewer bugs, and far better long-term maintainability.
- **File Content Caching**: Intelligent caching for file reads. Unmodified files are reused from cache — delivering major performance gains.
- **WebAssembly Support**: Built-in WASM instantiation and WAT-to-WASM compilation. Huge for portability and integration.
- **Expanded Arithmetic & Bitwise Operations**:
— Previously: `+ — × ÷ % **`
— Now adding: `|` (OR), `&` (AND), `~` (NOT), `^` (XOR), `<<` (SHIFT LEFT), `>>` (SHIFT RIGHT), `&^` (BIT CLEAR)
- **Fully Rewritten Runtime**: Cleaner architecture, better performance, and improved maintainability from the ground up.
- **Working FFI**: The Foreign Function Interface has been completely reworked and is now stable and reliable.
- **Cross-Platform Excellence**: Full native support for **Windows, macOS, and Linux**. Polished executable installers for macOS and Linux.
- **Embedded Standard Library**: The entire stdlib is now baked directly into the runtime — faster startup times and protection against tampering.
- **Keyword Updates** (cleaner and more familiar):
— `spawn` → `let`
— `immortal spawn` → `const`
— `static` has been removed entirely.
### The Bigger Vision
**ArachnoScript knows what it is. The language bends to what people need it to be.**
It is a powerful companion to JavaScript. With strong backend capabilities (servers, OS access, WASM, FFI), it pairs beautifully with JS frontends for modern full-stack development. Whether you’re building servers, tools, or experimenting with new ideas, ArachnoScript aims to make it safer and more enjoyable.
### Getting Started
Head over to the introductory website and follow the instructions on how to install the runtime:
→ [Introduction to ArachnoScript](https://buzzly.harislab.tech/t/introduction-to-arachnosript/228)
You can also join the conversation on [Buzzly](https://buzzly.harislab.tech/t/arachnoscript-v0-2-5-is-coming-soon/230) and [Discord](https://discord.gg/nQm7v2eXWt).
### What’s Next?
With v0.2.5 landing soon, the focus will shift toward performance (VM/JIT explorations), deeper WASM/FFI/Servers capabilities, and growing the ecosystem with community contributions.
I’m incredibly grateful to everyone who’s followed the journey, tried early builds, and given feedback. This project is still young, but the momentum feels real.
### Try It & Share Your Thoughts
What features would you like to see in future versions? Would you use ArachnoScript for backend work, scripting, or something else? Drop your thoughts in the comments — I read every one.
If you’re building something cool with it, tag me or share on Buzzly / Discord. Let’s push this forward together. 🕸️
#ArachnoScript #ProgrammingLanguages #WebAssembly #LanguageDesign #JavaScript #OpenSource #WebDevelopment #FFI
